one_time_description: Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council prior year deferred revenue recognized in current year
economic_notes: Nation has significant investment in Government Business Entities including Matchlee Limited Partnership (99.9% owned) and Matchlee General Partner Ltd (100% owned), plus 8 numbered BC companies. Equity income from GBEs was $2,173,774. Economic dependence noted on ISC funding through Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024 — document is for year ended March 31, 2023, signed March 26, 2024). Ten GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Prior period adjustment of $902,724 to housing accumulated amortization. Large prior year deferred revenue from NTC recognized in current year ($762,315). Assets held in trust decreased significantly from $5,693,314 to $462,080 (Ottawa Trust Funds revenue withdrawn). Resource Management segment had $3,119,759 in other income and recoveries — unusually large, possibly related to trust fund distributions. No schedule of remuneration provided. Social Development expenses of $467,192 are included within Health and Social Development segment expenses. Total government transfers calculated as: NTC $2,892,233 + Province of BC $2,787,864 + Government of Canada $354,385 + Fisheries and Oceans Canada $64,666 + First Nations Health Authority $357,880 + CMHC $169,331 + FNESC $59,212 + VIHA $0 + BC First Nations Gaming $407,385 + NTC prior year deferred $762,315 = $7,855,271 but BC First Nations Gaming may be OSR; conservative estimate excludes gaming. isc_funding set to NTC amount as ISC flows through NTC per Note 9.
